reviews.review_msg: Joao was a first-time prof, and I will say you could tell at times. But fundamentally he had the knowledge and care you want in a professor, his effort was there, and if you make an effort he not only responds, but is excited to talk about different topics. He could work on organization, but you gotta give him grace for his first semster
I love Dr. Bastos. Hes amazing. Hes a hard worker and I value everything he stands for. You should really take his class and you will learn so much.

these reviews seem a tad bit exaggerated; yes the lectures were boring+lengthy but a big part of it was that it was just simple/straightforward which is a shock since profs always give out a ton of info rather than keeping it simple. his midterms were quite easy, same as his mocks. he was a new prof so there were some hiccups, thoit wasnt hell

Dr Bastos is probably the kindest prof I have had at SFU. He is still new so you could tell he was trying to get a good feel of the lecture style at SFU/in Canada. I think a lot of the reviews here are a but extreme and biased just because people are mad at the curve (which is normal procedure to balance out grades within departments)

I feel like these ratings are a bit excessive, admd honestly compared to other HSCI profs I had in the past he's the most caring and flexible.He will change anything depending on the students feedback, he is pretty new and from Brazil so he isn't used to the class formats,but in general he was trying to learn his best!I really enjoyed him as a prof

joao is a new prof and it really shows. i wouldn't say avoid him at all costs but just know if lectures are bland and skippable. even though his ta wasn't whitney, they still marked like ass and was crazily unfair. exams are very similar to practices, but wording it a bit funky. i think it could've been better if he designed the course himself.

I'm so confused on why this professor was never prepared for anything? He went on long tangents that didn't even relate to what he was doing in class? The TAs marked like they had a personal vendetta against every student…

I am appalled at how this prof handled the course throughout the semester. The syllabus was outdated, and lectures were LENGTHY (he goes on long tangents on one topic, and it's not even explained well) with unnecessary activities (INSIDE THE LECTURE HALL W/ 70+ STUDENTS). Made the course content way more complicated than it actually is.

Probably the worst prof I have ever taken a class with, lectures were beyond boring. He doesnt know what he's doing half the time and it shows, so many things out of date with deadlines changed constantly. He tried to cater the last exam to our feedback to make it easier but ended curving the grade average a letter grade down! Clown show of a class

very timid. Felt a bit bad for him at times when we was trying to get the class's attention and nobody would listen because he was too quiet. His midterms are pretty much similar to the mock midterms he gives. Class is pretty straightforward.

I'm speechless at how incompetent a professor can be. Lectures were all over the place, and his exams are not even hard but insanely confusing. He tried his best to consider student feedback, so the final exam was made as easy as possible to boost everyone's grades, only to end up curving everyone's final grade down.

Disorganized prof. Minimal information on lecture slides, he was often unsure of what information was on the midterms even though it was his class. Deadlines were constantly changed with little notice, one was meant to be at the end of October but mid September decided it would be next week instead. Curves down.

AVOID! His syllabus was not up to date, and the deadlines were not accurate. His slides had very little info, and his lectures were sooooooo boring. He was so disorganized and couldn't seem to keep up with himself. The cherry on top was that he curved the class down, dropping me from A to B+.

most incompetent prof ever. bro didn't even know his own course had exams. said in the syllabus a whole presentation was due oct 30 and then tells us sept 18 that actually its due next week. half his emails are "i'd like to apologize for...". was really soft spoken and timid and unsure of himself which is really annoying as prof like stand up.
